name: Air Fryer Fried Chicken
description: Crispy, golden fried chicken made in the air fryer with a flavorful buttermilk marinade and seasoned breadcrumb coating — all the crunch with less oil.
instructions: Place the chicken pieces in a large zip-lock bag or bowl and pour the buttermilk over them. Seal and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or overnight for best results.
In a shallow dish, combine the flour, panko breadcrumbs, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Stir until well mixed.
Remove the chicken from the buttermilk, letting the excess drip off. Dredge each piece thoroughly in the seasoned flour mixture, pressing to adhere. Shake off any excess.
Preheat the air fryer to 375°F for 3 minutes.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray. Arrange the chicken pieces in a single layer, skin side up, without overcrowding. Spray the tops of the chicken with cooking spray.

Air fry at 375°F for 12 minutes. Flip the chicken pieces, spray again with cooking spray, and continue cooking for another 10–13 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 165°F and the coating is golden and crispy.
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes before serving.
notes: For extra crispy chicken, double-dip: after the first dredge, dip back in buttermilk and coat again. Do not overcrowd the basket — cook in batches if needed.

name: Chicken Chili
description: A hearty, warming white chicken chili loaded with tender shredded chicken, white beans, green chiles, and a creamy broth seasoned with cumin and chili powder.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 4–5 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook for 1 more minute.
Add the chicken breasts whole to the pot. Pour in the chicken broth, green chiles, cumin, chili powder, oregano, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 20–25 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the pot and shred it using two forks. Return the shredded chicken to the pot.
Add the drained white beans and cream cheese cubes to the pot. Stir over medium-low heat until the cream cheese is fully melted and incorporated.
Stir in the sour cream and simmer for another 5 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve topped with fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice. Optional toppings: shredded cheese, avocado slices, tortilla chips.
notes: This chili freezes well. For a thicker chili, mash some of the beans with a fork before adding them to the pot.

name: Chicken Enchiladas
description: Classic Mexican-style chicken enchiladas filled with seasoned shredded chicken and cheese, rolled in corn tortillas, and smothered in red enchilada sauce.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Lightly grease a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Sauté the onion for 3–4 minutes until soft. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Add the shredded chicken to the skillet along with cumin, chili powder, and salt. Pour in 0.25 cup of enchilada sauce and stir to combine. Cook for 2 minutes then remove from heat.
Warm the corn tortillas in the microwave wrapped in a damp paper towel (30–45 seconds) to make them pliable.
Spread 0.5 cup of enchilada sauce on the bottom of the prepared baking dish.
Place a spoonful of the chicken mixture and a sprinkle of cheese down the center of each tortilla. Roll tightly and place seam-side down in the baking dish.
Pour the remaining enchilada sauce evenly over the rolled tortillas. Top with the remaining shredded cheese.
Bake uncovered for 20–25 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the edges are slightly crispy.
Garnish with sour cream, fresh cilantro, and sliced green onions before serving.
notes: For easier rolling, briefly dip each corn tortilla in warm enchilada sauce before filling. Flour tortillas can be substituted for a softer texture.

name: Chicken Fajitas
description: Sizzling strips of marinated chicken breast with colorful bell peppers and onions, served in warm flour tortillas with all your favorite toppings.
instructions: In a bowl, combine 2 tablespoons olive oil, lime juice, chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Add the chicken strips and toss to coat. Marinate for at least 10 minutes (or up to 2 hours in the fridge).
Heat a large cast-iron skillet or heavy pan over high heat until very hot. Add 1 tablespoon olive oil.
Add the chicken strips in a single layer and cook for 3–4 minutes without stirring to get a good sear. Flip and cook another 2–3 minutes until cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced peppers and onions. Cook over high heat, stirring occasionally, for 4–5 minutes until softened and slightly charred.
Return the chicken to the skillet with the vegetables and toss together. Cook for 1 more minute.
Warm the flour tortillas in a dry skillet or microwave.
Serve the chicken and vegetables in the warm tortillas with sour cream, guacamole, salsa, and shredded cheese.
notes: For best results, use a very hot cast-iron skillet to achieve a good sear on the chicken. Chicken thighs can be substituted for juicier results.

name: Chicken Fricassee
description: A classic French braised chicken dish featuring tender chicken pieces simmered in a rich, creamy white wine and mushroom sauce with aromatic vegetables.
instructions: Pat the chicken pieces dry and season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon butter and the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the chicken on all sides, about 3–4 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons butter to the pot. Sauté the onion, celery, and carrots for 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook 1 more minute.
Add the mushrooms and cook for 4–5 minutes until they release their moisture and begin to brown.
Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and stir to coat. Cook for 1–2 minutes.
Pour in the white wine and stir, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Add the chicken broth, thyme sprigs, and bay leaf.
Return the chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the sauce. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook on low heat for 30–35 minutes until the chicken is very tender.
Remove the chicken. Discard the thyme sprigs and bay leaf. Stir in the heavy cream and lemon juice. Simmer uncovered for 5 minutes to thicken the sauce slightly.
Return the chicken to the sauce. Taste and adjust seasoning. Garnish with fresh parsley and serve over rice, egg noodles, or mashed potatoes.
notes: Fricassee is best made a day ahead as the flavors deepen overnight. Reheat gently over low heat. A splash of white wine can be added when reheating.

name: Chicken Gyros
description: Greek-inspired chicken gyros with juicy marinated grilled chicken, creamy tzatziki, fresh vegetables, and warm pita bread — a vibrant and satisfying meal.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, oregano, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Add the chicken thighs to the marinade and toss to coat. Marinate for at least 30 minutes (or up to 8 hours in the refrigerator).
Heat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat. Grill the chicken thighs for 5–6 minutes per side until cooked through and nicely charred. Internal temperature should reach 165°F.
Remove the chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes. Slice or chop into bite-sized pieces.
Warm the pita breads on the grill or in a dry skillet for about 1 minute per side.
Assemble the gyros: spread a generous amount of tzatziki on each warm pita. Top with sliced chicken, tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, parsley, and crumbled feta.
Wrap the pita around the filling and serve immediately.
notes: Store-bought tzatziki works great for a quick weeknight meal. For homemade tzatziki, combine Greek yogurt, grated cucumber (squeezed dry), garlic, dill, lemon juice, and salt.

name: Chicken Marsala
description: An Italian-American classic featuring pan-seared chicken cutlets in a rich Marsala wine and mushroom sauce — elegant enough for company, easy enough for a weeknight.
instructions: Season the flour with salt and pepper. Dredge each chicken breast in the seasoned flour, shaking off the excess.
Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil and 2 tablespoons butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown. Remove and set aside.

Reduce heat to medium. Add 1 tablespoon butter to the same skillet. Add the shallots and cook for 2 minutes. Add the mushrooms and cook for 5–6 minutes until they are golden and any released moisture has evaporated.
Add the garlic and fresh thyme and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the Marsala wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Let the wine reduce by half, about 3 minutes.
Add the chicken broth and heavy cream. Stir to combine and bring to a simmer.
Return the chicken to the pan, nestling it into the sauce. Simmer for 5 minutes, turning the chicken once, until the sauce has thickened and the chicken is heated through.
Finish with the remaining tablespoon of butter, stirring until melted into the sauce.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve immediately over pasta, mashed potatoes, or with crusty bread.
notes: Do not substitute cooking wine for dry Marsala — the flavor difference is significant. Sweet Marsala can also be used for a slightly richer sauce.

name: Chicken Noodle Soup
description: Classic homemade chicken noodle soup with tender chicken, hearty vegetables, and egg noodles in a golden, herb-seasoned broth — the ultimate comfort food.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the onion, carrots, and celery. Cook for 5–6 minutes until the vegetables begin to soften.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute more.
Add the chicken pieces, chicken broth, water, thyme sprigs, bay leaves, dried parsley, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25–30 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
Remove the chicken from the pot. Discard the thyme sprigs and bay leaves. When cool enough to handle, remove the chicken from the bones and shred or chop the meat into bite-sized pieces.
Return the shredded chicken to the pot. Bring the soup back to a gentle boil and add the egg noodles.
Cook the noodles for 8–10 minutes until tender. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley. Serve with crusty bread or crackers.
notes: If making the soup ahead, cook the noodles separately and add to individual bowls when serving — this prevents them from becoming mushy. The broth can be made a day ahead.

name: Chicken Parmesan
description: Tender breaded chicken cutlets topped with marinara sauce and melted mozzarella and Parmesan cheeses — a beloved Italian-American classic the whole family will love.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Set up a breading station with three shallow dishes: flour (seasoned with a pinch of salt and pepper), beaten eggs, and the breadcrumbs mixed with 0.25 cup Parmesan and garlic powder.
Season the chicken cutlets with salt and pepper. Dredge in flour, dip in egg, then press firmly into the breadcrumb mixture to coat on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the breaded chicken for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown. Work in batches to avoid crowding.
Transfer all the chicken back to the skillet (or a baking dish). Spoon marinara sauce over each piece.
Top with shredded mozzarella and the remaining Parmesan.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15–18 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the chicken is cooked through (internal temperature 165°F).
Garnish with fresh basil leaves and serve immediately over spaghetti or with a green salad and crusty bread.
notes: For extra crispy chicken, use panko breadcrumbs in place of regular Italian breadcrumbs. Avoid adding too much sauce before baking or the coating can become soggy.

name: Chicken Pot Pie
description: A comforting, golden-crusted chicken pot pie filled with tender chicken, vegetables, and a creamy herb-seasoned sauce — a true homestyle classic.

ingredients.name: refrigerated pie crusts
ingredients.prepNote: one for bottom, one for top

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Melt butter in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion, carrots, and celery and cook for 6–7 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and stir to coat. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Gradually whisk in the chicken broth, then the milk. Bring to a simmer, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens, about 4–5 minutes.
Stir in the cooked chicken, frozen peas, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper. Remove from heat and let the filling cool slightly.
Press one pie crust into the bottom and sides of a 9-inch deep-dish pie plate.

Pour the warm filling into the crust. Place the second pie crust on top, trim the edges, and crimp the edges of the top and bottom crusts together to seal. Cut several small slits in the top crust to vent.
Brush the top crust with the beaten egg wash.
Place the pie on a baking sheet to catch any drips. Bake for 30–35 minutes until the crust is deep golden brown.
Let the pot pie cool for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
notes: The filling can be made up to 2 days ahead and refrigerated. Let the filling come to room temperature before adding to the crust. Cover the crust edges with foil if they brown too quickly.
